title: Fisherman Song
description: A fun calypso song about going fishing in Trinidad and Tobago with family.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Sunrise peekin', sky so bright,
Time for fishin', what a delight!
Grab mi rod, and reel so new,
Fishin' wid family, me and you!

[Chorus]
Row, row, row de boat so fine,
Catch a fish, dat will be divine!
[CLAP] Pull de line, [SNAP] feel de tug,
Fisherman's song, it sounds so snug!

[Verse 2]
Down by de beach, de water clear,
Little fishes swimmin' near!
Bait on de hook, throw it wide,
Waitin' patient, side by side!

[Chorus]
Row, row, row de boat so fine,
Catch a fish, dat will be divine!
[CLAP] Pull de line, [SNAP] feel de tug,
Fisherman's song, it sounds so snug!

[Verse 3]
Ahoy! Ahoy! A pull so strong,
Dis fish is dancin' all along!
We reel it in, with happy glee,
Look what we catch, for all to see!

[Chorus]
Row, row, row de boat so fine,
Catch a fish, dat will be divine!
[CLAP] Pull de line, [SNAP] feel de tug,
Fisherman's song, it sounds so snug!

[Verse 4]
Dat ting nice! (That thing is nice!) Fish for dinner soon,
We sing and dance beneath de moon!
Tankful for de sea so deep,
Good memories we will always keep!
thumbnailPrompt: Vibrant illustration for a children's song about fishing in Trinidad and Tobago. Depict a family of four (two parents, two children) in a colorful fishing boat on turquoise water. The children are holding fishing rods and smiling. The father is rowing the boat, and the mother is pointing at a fish jumping out of the water. The background features a tropical island with lush green hills, palm trees, and a bright blue sky. Include musical notes swirling around the boat and incorporate traditional Caribbean patterns and colors. Include the instruments Steelpan and the Shakers around the boat
educationalValue: This song teaches children about the Caribbean tradition of fishing, family time, and being grateful for nature. It also introduces a simple Trinidadian Patois phrase 'Dat ting nice!' meaning 'That thing is nice!'
